---
title: "About the JS library"
author: "Nicolas Lambert"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{About the JS library}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

<img src="../man/figures/js.png" 
     style="width:170px; margin: 0 auto; border:none;" 
     alt="Logo">

The R package geoviz is a partial implementation of the JavaScript geoviz library. To understand how it works under the hood, we invite you to take a look at this JS library.

# Source code

The source code is hosted on GitHub. Development is active.

👉 https://github.com/riatelab/geoviz

# API Documentation

The library documentation is available here. It allows you to see all the parameters.

👉 https://riatelab.github.io/geoviz/

# Live documentation

The documentation is also available through live examples on the Observable platform. Click on the thumbnails to access the desired technical sheet.

```{=html}
<div id="observablehq-documentation-1d5d505b"></div>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/inspector@5/dist/inspector.css">
<script type="module">
import {Runtime, Inspector} from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/runtime@5/dist/runtime.js";
import define from "https://api.observablehq.com/@neocartocnrs/geoviz-appendix.js?v=4";
new Runtime().module(define, name => {
  if (name === "documentation") return new Inspector(document.querySelector("#observablehq-documentation-1d5d505b"));
});
</script>
```

# Examples

You will also find numerous map examples along with their associated code.

```{=html}
<div id="observablehq-gallery-28e37508"></div>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/inspector@5/dist/inspector.css">
<script type="module">
import {Runtime, Inspector} from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/runtime@5/dist/runtime.js";
import define from "https://api.observablehq.com/@neocartocnrs/geoviz-appendix.js?v=4";
new Runtime().module(define, name => {
  if (name === "gallery") return new Inspector(document.querySelector("#observablehq-gallery-28e37508"));
});
</script>
```

# The "regard de cartographe"

This `geoviz` JavaScript library is used to produce an interactive map every month in the French newspaper l'Humanité. The section can be found here:

👉 https://www.humanite.fr/serie/le-regard-du-cartographe
